detail.note: All primary and supporting resources cited at least once. Key factual claims across §5, §6, §7 carry [N] markers at well above the 60% threshold.

soft_findings.note: Used in §5.3 with a back-reference '(the context window you met in 3.2)'. The term is not in 3.2's formal concepts_introduced list in sequence.index.json, but is clearly an LLM-architecture concept from 3.2 and is correctly attributed as prior knowledge rather than introduced fresh.

Listed in 3.3 concepts_introduced in sequence.index.json but never defined or mentioned in the corpus body. The concept is absent, creating a gap between the declared concepts_introduced and actual corpus coverage.

findings.detail: Estimated ~2960 words is approximately 40 words (1.3%) below the 3000-word standard floor. Well within the 30% failure tolerance (floor for hard fail = 2100 words). Recommend builder pad §5.4, §8, or §9 by one paragraph to clear the floor cleanly.

topic_scope.detail: No out-of-scope material found. LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI appear in §6 as a single one-liner with a 'covered in later topics' pointer — within the allowed one-sentence defer rule. No multi-agent systems content, no RAG, no attention-mechanism content. The §6 Implementation section is conceptual scaffolding for the agent loop and remains within scope.

findings.detail: 'API' is used in §5.3 (tool use bullet list: 'API calls — talk to external services') without spelling out 'Application Programming Interface'. The inline gloss '...talk to external services (book a flight, send an email, read a database)' partially compensates, but the acronym itself is never expanded.

'orchestration' is declared in the topic's concepts_introduced (sequence.index.json) but does not appear anywhere in the corpus body. Beginners completing this topic will not have encountered the concept despite the curriculum expecting it to be introduced here.

beginner_readability.note: 2 soft findings, below the ≥3 threshold for escalation to hard fail. Readability is generally excellent — plain register, analogies used well, terms defined on first use throughout.

verdict: PASS. All six checks pass. Three soft findings that do not individually or collectively trigger a hard fail: (1) word count ~2960 is ~40 words below the 3000-word standard floor but within the 30% tolerance — builder should add one short paragraph to §5.4 or §8 to clear the floor; (2) 'API' acronym is used in §5.3 without expansion — add '(Application Programming Interface)' on first use; (3) 'orchestration' is listed in this topic's concepts_introduced in sequence.index.json but is never defined or mentioned in the corpus body — either define it briefly (one sentence) in §5.3 or §5.2, or remove it from concepts_introduced if the sequencing team decides it belongs later. No hard forward references, no scope creep, no citation gaps, no jargon dumps, no fake citations.
